title: Backup, Point-in-Time Recovery, Rebuild, and Disaster Reconciliation · Language Lens: Feed Go Disaster Drill
summary: Backups exist but are untested, feed content is assumed refetchable, reader state shares no recovery objective, restore replays workers early, and corruption remains hidden. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go disaster drill increment in rehearsed feed-service recovery system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Rehearse region loss, corruption, accidental deletion, bad migration, credential leak, publisher incident, and partial restore with owned timelines.
Define migration, database restore, secret rotation, scheduler pause, validator review, worker resume, cache rebuild, and client reopening order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Disaster Drill · Theory Feed Go Disaster Drill
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6814 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove data-class objectives, backup eligibility, isolated restore, safe resume order, and disaster drills; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feed Go Disaster Drill contract for the supplied data.
